full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Do what?<|4.08|> <|5.14|> Love?<|5.90|> <|6.16|> Who?<|6.44|> <|8.28|> I must admit, this teaching from Jesus calling us to do to others as you would have them do<|15.42|> <|15.42|> to you is really, really quite difficult when it involves your enemies, right?<|21.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Do good to your haters.<|2.20|> <|2.50|> Bless your cursors.<|3.70|> <|4.16|> Pray for your abusers.<|5.50|> <|6.10|> You are kidding me.<|7.50|> <|8.56|> But it gets even richer.<|9.80|> <|10.42|> Offer the other cheek.<|11.54|> <|12.40|> Don't hold back your shirt.<|13.96|> <|14.18|> Give to beggars.<|15.28|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> What appears to be key here in my reading of the text is the word "anyone."<|6.00|> <|6.00|> Jesus seems to be saying there are no exceptions to be made among who he commands you do good to,<|14.00|> <|14.00|> which in a rules-based world seems contradictory at best.<|20.00|> <|20.00|> In all, Christ demands love and forbids spite or retaliation.<|28.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> In growing up and searching for my faith, this was a central point of examination.<|8.60|> <|8.60|> I wrestled with the big questions, and as I had difficult experiences with people along<|15.68|> <|15.68|> the road of life, I discovered how hard it is to love people.<|22.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> to love my bullies in grade school,<|3.76|> <|3.76|> my abusive ex-girlfriend,<|6.26|> <|6.26|> and my father who divorced my mother.<|9.76|> <|9.76|> I also struggled to find love for myself,<|14.90|> <|14.90|> love despite my sins and shortcomings,<|19.46|> <|19.46|> my selfishness, my desire,<|23.14|> <|23.14|> my run from God.<|26.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> My journey toward faith in Christ was to put together these broken pieces of the soul with<|9.00|> <|9.00|> the glue of an irresistible message that called for loving God, loving neighbor, and loving<|16.68|> <|16.68|> myself.<|19.14|> <|19.14|> It was only through reunion with Christ, as Richard mentioned this morning, that this<|25.46|> <|25.46|> ever felt truly possible.<|28.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> a tugging on the heart unstoppable.<|5.00|> <|5.00|> For Jesus gave his life for us.<|10.28|> <|10.28|> He taught, he shared,<|13.58|> <|13.58|> he led by example and was killed because of it.<|17.72|> <|17.72|> He died an awful death on a cross by those we would consider enemies,<|24.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And instead of condemnation and revenge, he returned resurrected so that we might live.<|9.32|> <|10.70|> Live beyond our human nature and beyond our violence.<|15.26|> <|16.56|> Live into a love beyond our wildest imaginations.<|21.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> and the teachings.<|2.20|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The message of the gospel contrasts with the ways of the world.<|6.32|> <|7.82|> We've seen it throughout human history, and unfortunately, especially right now.<|12.88|> <|14.78|> Revenge and resentment are condoned or worse, encouraged.<|18.42|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The gap between the economically rich and poor grows wider.<|4.88|> <|6.00|> Friends are betrayed, alliances in doubt, and the forces of evil are on the move.<|11.36|> <|12.80|> It is a time like this when we need Jesus' teachings more than ever to set ourselves right.<|21.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We need forgiveness and charity, non-judgment and kindness, goodness and mercy.<|8.80|> <|10.00|> Mercy, mercy, mercy.<|13.40|> <|15.54|> In the search for spiritual encouragement, one place I look is among the people here.<|24.42|> <|24.42|> Amen.<|25.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> A woman who bravely testified against a doctor who sexually assaulted her.<|7.64|> <|7.64|> A man who pours over deadly car accident videos in order to make our roadways safer.<|16.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The children and teens who proudly serve at our cathedral nights as cooks and doing the dishes, that's big.<|9.96|> <|11.34|> And the transgender person who finally, finally feels safe being exactly who they are here at the cathedral.<|20.86|> <|22.08|> Amen.<|22.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|3.36|> These examples remind me of why I am here,<|7.86|> <|7.86|> who I belong to and who I listen.<|11.16|> <|13.14|> God in Christ liberates us from our sin and wickedness.<|18.14|> <|19.98|> He saves us from our worst selves<|23.48|> <|23.48|> and invites us to become our best.<|26.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> He does not forsake us in our darkest hour, but restores us to life evermore.<|6.70|> <|8.34|> The question then, children of God, children of the Most High,<|14.20|> <|15.10|> is what loving actions can we take in our lives to model Christ and transform the human heart?<|24.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> For me, I believe it is time to be in a faith community, to pray for all people, to be in<|12.74|> <|12.74|> relationship and to support each other, speak out against evil and injustice, and serve<|20.84|> <|20.84|> in the world.<|21.88|> <|21.88|> Amen.<|22.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> For me, it is time to be prophetic.<|4.46|> <|5.82|> It is time to be a Christian.<|8.72|> <|11.18|> Do what?<|12.90|> <|13.68|> Love who?<|15.46|> <|17.58|> Amen.<|18.42|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|0.74|><|endoftext|>
